description.task: Implement a SimpleStack class using an int array. The class should support push, pop, peek, isEmpty, and size operations. Demonstrate LIFO behavior by pushing a sequence and then popping until empty.
instructions: push(val): if top == data.length-1 throw new RuntimeException('Stack overflow'); else data[++top]=val
pop(): if top == -1 throw new RuntimeException('Stack underflow'); else return data[top--]
peek() and isEmpty() and size() are already implemented
description.hint: push: if(top==data.length-1) throw new RuntimeException('Stack overflow'); data[++top]=val; pop: if(top==-1) throw new RuntimeException('Stack underflow'); return data[top--];
description.expectedOutput:
Peek: 30
Size: 3
Pop: 30
Pop: 20
Pop: 10
Empty: true

description.task: Implement a MinStack class that supports push, pop, peek, and getMin — all in O(1) time. Use an auxiliary stack to track the minimum at each level.
instructions: push: after pushing to main stack, push Math.min(val, minStack.isEmpty()?val:minStack.peek()) to minStack
pop: pop from both stack and minStack
After pop() (removes 2): getMin=3; after another pop() (removes 7): getMin=3; peek=3
description.hint: push minStack: int m = minStack.isEmpty() ? val : Math.min(val, minStack.peek()); minStack.push(m);
description.expectedOutput:
Min: 2
Min after pop: 3
Min after pop: 3
Peek: 3

description.task: Implement a BalancedParentheses class that uses a stack to check if a string of brackets is balanced. Support three types: (), [], and {}. Return true if balanced, false otherwise.
instructions: For each character: if '(', '[', or '{' push onto stack
If ')', ']', or '}': if stack is empty return false; pop top and check isMatch(top, c); if not match return false
description.hint: if(c=='('||c=='['||c=='{') stack.push(c); else { if(stack.isEmpty()) return false; char top=stack.pop(); if(!isMatch(top,c)) return false; }
